steps.title: What is an x-intercept?
steps.content: An x-intercept occurs where the graph crosses the x-axis, meaning y = 0. We need to find the point(s) where the parabola meets the horizontal line y = 0.

steps.title: Reading the crossing points from the graph
steps.content: Tracing the parabola from left to right, it crosses the x-axis at two locations: one at x = 4 and another at x = 6. The vertex is at approximately (5, 1), which lies above the x-axis between these two crossings.

steps.title: Identifying the correct choice
steps.content:
Among the given choices, (4, 0) is visible as the left crossing point on the graph.

Checking alternatives: choices A and C involve negative x-values not shown on the visible domain; choice B, (5, 0), would require the parabola to pass through the vertex with y = 0, but the vertex has y ≈ 1 instead.

The point (4, 0) matches choice D — D is correct.
